strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use ROP to call the system() function with the argument '/bin/sh' to get a shell. We need to find the offset to control the return address, and we already have the pop_rdi_ret gadget. We will also need the address of '/bin/sh' in the binary or libc.
